summary: Iteration 4 comprehensive end-to-end verification of new modules — 32/32 pytest backend tests PASS, all 6 new frontend pages load and interactive flows work. Verified: 7-role migration + login for all seeded users (super_admin/admin/dealer/sales/presales/consultant/customer), GET /api/meta/roles returns 7 roles, 9 Novastar controllers seeded and auto-planner recommends VX600/H2 for 3M pixels and multi-unit MX40 Pro for >26M pixels, engineering endpoints (power/network/structural) return correct math (three-phase 15kW ≈ 24A → MCB 32A; 4M pixels → 7 home runs CAT6; 40 cabinets @ 9sqm → M12 bolts), 10-column kanban Projects pipeline with create/move/delete, AI Content generator produced 9307-char LLM output, AI Render (Nano Banana gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview) returned real file_id + url on live call. Existing endpoints (products, customers, partners, quotes, configure, dashboard) all pass regression. Sidebar shows Overview/Configurator/Sales/AI Studio/Admin sections with all 15 nav items visible.
verified_endpoints: POST /api/auth/login for admin2/dealer/sales/presales/consultant/customer → 200 with matching new roles
POST /api/controllers/plan {total_pixels:3000000} → recommended VX600 or H2 with ports_used, ports_free, utilization_percent, note
POST /api/controllers/plan {total_pixels:50000000} → recommended MX40 Pro with quantity>=2
POST /api/engineering/power {power_max_kw:15, phase:'three', daily_hours:10} → voltage=400V, current_max≈24A, mcb=32A, rccb 30mA, cable_size, ups_kva, generator_kva, monthly_kwh, monthly_cost_inr
POST /api/engineering/network {cabinets_x:8, cabinets_y:5, total_pixels:4000000, controller_ports:16} → home_runs=7, cable_type=CAT6 UTP, cable_length, signal_flow list
POST /api/engineering/structural {total_weight_kg:320, wall 4000x2250, 40 cab, wall_mount} → anchor_bolt_count>=6 (M12 spec), recommended_structure, load_per_sqm
POST /api/admin/users → create user w/ role=sales; PUT /api/admin/users/{id} updates role+active; DELETE /api/admin/users/{self.id} → 400 (self-delete blocked)
POST /api/ai/render {scene:'meeting_room', wall_width_m:4, wall_height_m:2.25} → 200 {file_id, url, scene, prompt} (Nano Banana file stored to Emergent object storage)
GET /api/ai/render/scenes → 11 scenes incl. meeting_room, auditorium, dooh, control_room
Regression: GET /api/products, /api/customers, /api/partners, /api/quotes, /api/dashboard/stats, POST /api/configure — all 200 with expected shape
verified_ui_flows: Sidebar shows all 5 sections (Overview / Configurator / Sales / AI Studio / Admin) with 15 nav items each visible (dashboard-nav, configurator-nav, controllers-nav, engineering-nav, quotes-nav, projects-nav, customers-nav, partners-nav, ai-nav, ai-render-nav, ai-content-nav, products-nav, users-nav, reports-nav, settings-nav)
/users (users-page): lists all users; new-user-btn opens dialog; created UI_Test user via user-name/email/password inputs + user-role-select (Dealer) + save-user-btn → row appears in users-table
/controllers (controllers-page): 9 Novastar cards rendered with model/class/max_pixels/ports/price; planner-pixels-input=3000000 + planner-run-btn → planner-result includes VX600/H2 recommendation

/engineering (engineering-page): 3 tabs (tab-power/tab-network/tab-structural) all clickable; power-calc-btn → shows MCB/UPS stats; network-calc-btn → shows CAT6/home runs; structural-calc-btn → shows anchor bolts

/projects (projects-page): 10-column kanban visible; new-project-btn → dialog with project-name-input + project-customer-select; save-project-btn creates card in kanban; move-forward-{id} advances stage
/ai-content (ai-content-page): content-type-select + content-context-input + content-run-btn → generated 9307-char draft in content-output
/ai-render (ai-render-page): scene-select + generate button (test scene loads; live render also succeeded via backend curl)
/configurator (regression): loads correctly, BOQ + Grand Total visible, all prior include toggles + spare percent present
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 122/130/137/143 still references legacy role 'product_manager' in require_roles for /api/products (create/update/delete/duplicate). After the role migration this role no longer exists — only super_admin can now manage products. The seeded admin2@logic.com/admin (role=admin) cannot create/edit/delete products. If admin should manage products, change require_roles('product_manager','super_admin') → require_roles('admin','super_admin').

server.py line 192/237/337 still references legacy role 'sales_manager' in require_roles (delete customer/partner/quote). Since seed migrated 'sales_manager' → 'sales', delete permission is effectively locked to super_admin only, not sales as PRD may intend. Consider updating to require_roles('super_admin','admin','sales').

server.py line 291 special-cases user['role'] == 'sales_executive' for quote visibility. After migration this role no longer exists — the branch is dead code. Update to 'sales' (or 'sales' + 'dealer' + 'presales' + 'consultant' per PRD) to correctly scope non-manager quote visibility.
server.py: api2 defines POST /api/upload with `Query` imported twice (line 767) — cosmetic redundant import but harmless.

AiStudio.jsx line 25: api.get(render.url.replace('/api','')) — since api baseURL already ends with '/api' this correctly strips the prefix, but the URL manipulation is fragile. Consider returning full URL from backend or using a dedicated /api/files/{id} axios call directly.

storage.py line 15: init_storage() is called at every put_object/get_object via memoized _storage_key global. If EMERGENT_LLM_KEY rotates at runtime, the cached key becomes stale until backend restart. Not blocking today.

roles.py PERMISSIONS matrix is defined but not referenced in server.py anywhere — permission gates still use require_roles() with role names. Either wire has_permission() in as a dependency, or remove PERMISSIONS to avoid confusion.

structural.py line 44: maintenance_front_mm logic uses `'front' in mount_type` which will always be False for the 3 defined mount types (wall_mount / hanging / floor_stand) — the branch is unreachable, defaults to 400. Consider making this an explicit field or removing the dead branch.
test_credentials: admin@logic.com/admin123 (super_admin), admin2@logic.com/admin2123 (admin), sales@logic.com/sales123 (sales), presales@logic.com/presales123 (presales), dealer@logic.com/dealer123 (dealer), consultant@logic.com/consultant123 (consultant), customer@logic.com/customer123 (customer) — all verified working
seed_data_creation: Used existing seed. Test-created data (TEST_ITER4 project, test_iter4@logic.com user, UI_TEST_ITER4 project, ui_test_iter4@logic.com user) all cleaned up in teardown / evaluate blocks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-4 endpoints/pages verified. Backend AI budget is currently healthy — both /api/ai/content and /api/ai/render returned real 200 responses (not 402). If future runs hit 402, the endpoint's error handling is intentional and should be treated as PASS per problem statement. Note: three legacy role references in server.py (product_manager, sales_manager, sales_executive) remain and gate certain endpoints incorrectly after role migration — reported in critical_code_review_comments. All 15 sidebar data-testids are correctly wired.
